description: A vertical (portrait) designed infographic/slogan card with a very dark navy-blue background. A faint, low-opacity background image of a construction crane/lattice tower and a partially visible American flag is layered behind the text. The header is large white serif display type reading 'DEPARTMENT OF LABOR' (DEPARTMENT OF on the first line, LABOR enlarged on the second). Below is a yellow/gold bold sans subhead 'Tax Day Wins' with a gold underline rule. Three bullet points each marked by a white checkmark are listed in white bold sans text: (1) 'The Working Families Tax Cut includes No Tax on Overtime and No Tax on Tips.' (2) 'No Tax on Tips: Claimed so far on six million tax returns filed in 2026.' (3) 'No Tax on Overtime: Claimed so far on 25 million tax returns filed in 2026.' At the bottom center is a small white line-art icon of the White House building. The style is official, polished government-comms branding. Messaging technique: a 'wins' checklist touting Trump tax policy achievements with concrete-sounding statistics. Target audience: working/middle-class taxpayers, framed around Tax Day. Note: branded as Department of Labor content but posted from the White House account.
notable_text: DEPARTMENT OF LABOR; Tax Day Wins; No Tax on Tips: six million tax returns; No Tax on Overtime: 25 million tax returns filed in 2026
